# nxos_udld (Resource)

This resource can manage the UDLD configuration on NX-OS devices, including global settings and per-interface UDLD configuration such as aggressive mode and bidirectional detection.

### API Documentation

- [udldEntity](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/Discovery%20Protocols/udld:Entity/)
- [udldInst](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/Discovery%20Protocols/udld:Inst/)
- [udldPhysIf](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/Discovery%20Protocols/udld:PhysIf/)

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nxos_udld" "example" {
  admin_state      = "enabled"
  aggressive       = "enabled"
  message_interval = 20
  interfaces = {
    "eth1/9" = {
      admin_state             = "port-enabled"
      aggressive              = "enabled"
      bidirectional_detection = "port-enabled"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `admin_state` (String) The administrative state of the object or policy.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `aggressive` (String) Global Aggressive Mode.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.
- `interfaces` (Attributes Map) List of UDLD interfaces.
  - Map key: `interface_id` - Must match first field in the output of `show intf brief`. Example: `eth1/1`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--interfaces))
- `message_interval` (Number) Probe Message Interval.
  - Range: `7`-`90`

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The distinguished name of the object.

<a id="nestedatt--interfaces"></a>
### Nested Schema for `interfaces`

Optional:

- `admin_state` (String) UDLD enable/disable per port.
  - Choices: `port-enabled`, `port-fiber-disabled`, `port-default`, `port-default-disable`
- `aggressive` (String) Interface Aggressive Mode.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `bidirectional_detection` (String) Bidirectional Detection Enabled.
  - Choices: `port-enabled`, `port-fiber-disabled`, `port-default`, `port-default-disable`

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to       = nxos_udld.example
  identity = {}
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema


#### Optional

- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = nxos_udld.example
  id = ""
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import nxos_udld.example ""
```
